text:
Market today: Mixed signals are at play. Positive AI sentiment (Palantir) offers some tailwind, but Tesla's drop despite strong deliveries suggests underlying caution or profit-taking in growth names. Trump's tech buys could signal broader institutional interest. This creates a volatile environment for your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL is well below its stop at -28.7% and is a leveraged ETF; this needs immediate attention. QQQI is near its stop at -1.86%, consider trimming as planned. SPAL is a new, leveraged ETF, already down -9.58% – remember margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep an eye on SMH, your chip ETF, for a potential pullback to the scanner's buy zone. Also, monitor general tech sentiment given the mixed headlines.

Bottom line: Manage your leveraged losing positions aggressively, especially with margin amplifying risk.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 was breached. Consider cutting this leveraged position to prevent further decay and capital drain.
QQQI: It's close to your 52 stop. Trim as planned if it hits that level to manage risk on this income anchor.
SMH: If it pulls back to the scanner's entry zone (586.43-584.71), consider adding to your existing position.
